scoring_note: S2 citation counts unavailable (API 429); novelty judged against literature_pool contents incl. phase4_recheck prior-art fetches. Budget basis: 4xL40-48GB, 600 GPU-h gate, deadline 2026-09-24.

candidates.title: How Much Thinking Does Seeing Need? Adaptive-Depth Latent Visual Reasoning with Evidence-Sufficiency Halting
candidates.pain_point: Every multimodal latent-reasoning method (CoLT, DLWM, Latent Visual Reasoning) uses a FIXED latent length / uniform computation budget regardless of instance difficulty; reviewers flag this and also that evaluations never isolate 'thinking in latents' from 'extra fine-tuning' (LVR review: the text-only FT stage drives most of the gain). Meanwhile decode-side token generation, not vision encoding, dominates real latency/energy (arxiv:2607.09520).
candidates.key_intuition: Visual instances carry variable perceptual ambiguity (occlusion/clutter/ambiguity per DLWM), so required latent computation should be instance-dependent: learn a halting head over latent steps driven by an evidence-sufficiency signal, so easy instances exit after 1-2 latent steps and hard ones think longer. Second intuition: with matched-data/matched-compute controls (latent steps vs equivalent extra text tokens vs no-op padding steps), we can finally measure whether latent visual thinking itself helps — the control protocol is a contribution reviewers explicitly asked for.
candidates.expected_baseline: CoLT (arxiv:2606.31986) fixed-length latent CoT + explicit-CoT and direct-answer baselines on the same backbone (Qwen2.5-VL-3B/7B-LoRA)
candidates.closest_prior_art_and_difference: TARPO (arxiv:2606.05859) routes latent-vs-explicit per token for TEXT LLM RL; ALAR (arxiv:2606.02871) escalates latent->CoT across AGENT turns; Think-Less-Act-Early (arxiv:2606.15099) early-exits in VLA action models. None does adaptive DEPTH of latent visual reasoning in general VLMs, none grounds halting in visual-evidence sufficiency, and none provides the confound-controlled evaluation protocol.
candidates.novelty_justification: Substantive-contribution band: new mechanism (evidence-sufficiency halting for latent visual thought) + a controlled protocol the subfield's reviewers demanded. Held below 0.7 because adaptive latent computation exists in adjacent settings (text RL, agents, VLA) — the domain-specific mechanism (perceptual-ambiguity-driven halting) is what keeps it above the 0.4 'X applied to Y' cap.
candidates.feasibility_justification: LoRA on Qwen2.5-VL-3B (full) and 7B (LoRA): ~15-25 GPU-h per training run. Main (3 seeds) + 4 ablations (halting off / fixed-depth grid / padding control / text-token control, 3 seeds each at 3B) + eval passes ~= 330 GPU-h x1.3 buffer ~= 430 GPU-h of 600 (72%) — 'tight fit' band, mitigated by doing ablations at 3B only.
candidates.contribution_shape_justification: Mechanism + falsifiable claims + a methodological protocol; not a benchmark sweep. The controlled-evaluation component is framed as supporting the mechanism, not as an eval paper.
candidates.venue_fit_justification: ICLR's core: representation learning + adaptive computation + honest-measurement story; fits 9 pages; fully open-model reproducible.
risk_factors: Latent-CoT training recipes are young; reproduction of CoLT-class baseline may take 2-3 debug cycles
Halting head may collapse to always-max-depth (mitigation: depth-cost penalty, curriculum)
If latent reasoning fails to beat matched-compute text controls at 3B, headline becomes a (still publishable, but riskier) negative/measurement result
MazePlanning/spatial suites may be too easy at 7B, too hard at 3B — dataset triage needed in Stage 2

candidates.title: Where Does RL Actually Help? Interventional Perception-vs-Reasoning Error Accounting for Multimodal RLVR
candidates.pain_point: Multimodal RLVR papers (Perception-R1, Revisual-R1, CFPO) report aggregate gains; reviewers explicitly ask 'what fraction of baseline failures are perception errors, and which of those does the method fix?' and 'does text-heavy RL degrade visual grounding?' — no paper provides an identifiable error decomposition or longitudinal accounting across RL training.
candidates.key_intuition: Oracle-perception interventions (swap raw image for ground-truth structured perception: gold captions/detections/scene graphs) give an identifiable per-instance decomposition of failure into perception vs reasoning components. Tracking this accounting across GRPO training checkpoints reveals WHAT RL changes; hypothesis (from PAPO's observation + Revisual reviews): RLVR mostly reshapes reasoning while leaving perception errors intact or worse — then a perception-preserving constraint (grounding-regression penalty) targeted by the accounting recovers the gap.
candidates.expected_baseline: PAPO (arxiv:2507.06448) implicit-perception-loss GRPO + plain GRPO on Qwen2.5-VL-3B
candidates.closest_prior_art_and_difference: PAPO observes perception is a major error source and adds a KL loss, but provides no identifiable decomposition estimator, no longitudinal accounting of what RL changes, and no forgetting audit. Perception-R1 (openreview:KttCXdjj4w) rewards perception but reviewers note attribution confusion with GRPO. We contribute the measurement framework + causal findings + a targeted fix validated by the framework.
candidates.novelty_justification: Meaningful-delta-to-substantive band: the interventional accounting framework and longitudinal findings are new; capped by PAPO already occupying 'perception matters in multimodal RL' and by the fix being a loss-term modification.
candidates.feasibility_justification: Diagnosis phase is inference-only over public checkpoints (cheap, ~60 GPU-h). But the longitudinal + intervention phases need our own GRPO runs: 3B model, LoRA, modest rollouts on 4xL40 without NVLink ~= 25-35 GPU-h per run x (2 methods x 3 seeds + 2 ablations) x1.3 ~= 320-390 GPU-h; plus RL training instability risk on tight interconnect. 'Tight fit' with real schedule risk.
candidates.contribution_shape_justification: Hypothesis-driven intervention study with a mechanism fix; must be kept from drifting into 'an evaluation of RL methods' — the accounting must serve the causal claim and the fix.
candidates.venue_fit_justification: ICLR rewards analysis-plus-fix papers; directly answers questions its own reviewers asked last cycle.
risk_factors: GRPO rollout throughput on 4xL40 (48G, PCIe) for a 3B VLM is unproven — Stage 2 must pilot before committing
Oracle-perception annotations exist only for some benchmarks (need datasets with gold captions/detections — e.g. COCO-based VQA); dataset triage critical
If RLVR turns out to fix perception errors too, headline weakens to a nuanced accounting (less exciting, still defensible)
Crowded fast-moving RLVR space; risk of being scooped between now and September

candidates.title: Commitment Points: When Multimodal Reasoning Locks In a Hallucination, and Cheap Deferral at the Lock-In Step
candidates.pain_point: Hallucination mitigation intervenes uniformly at decoding while reviewers note the causal story is missing and side effects (refusal up, OOD accuracy down — CASAL review) and overhead (Token-Guard/HalluGuard reviews) go unmeasured. HIVE (arxiv:2607.07507) shows hallucinated semantics persist and contaminate downstream reasoning, but offers evaluation only — no account of WHEN during a multimodal chain the model commits to a hallucination, nor a mitigation targeted at that step.
candidates.key_intuition: If hallucinations 'lock in' at identifiable commitment steps (attractor-like dynamics shown for text LLMs in arxiv:2604.15400), then intervening ONLY at detected commitment points (targeted re-grounding / deferral to visual evidence) should beat uniform contrastive-decoding baselines at a fraction of the overhead, with a measurable side-effect profile. The commitment-point analysis doubles as the paper's scientific finding.
candidates.expected_baseline: Uniform decoding-time mitigations: contrastive/visual-contrastive decoding class + Token-Guard-style self-checking (openreview:5fCDEz43ya) on LLaVA-1.5-7B and Qwen2.5-VL-7B
candidates.closest_prior_art_and_difference: arxiv:2604.15400 establishes commitment/attractor dynamics for TEXT LLMs; arxiv:2605.13156 and arxiv:2606.08777 do circuit-level causal analysis of VLM hallucination but neither temporal commitment during multimodal CoT nor a commitment-targeted mitigation with side-effect/overhead audit.
candidates.novelty_justification: Meaningful-delta band: temporal-commitment analysis in VLM reasoning chains + targeted-intervention mechanism is a real step, but the causal-hallucination space is crowded (2605.13156, 2606.08777, 2606.27596, 2604.15400 all within 4 months) — honest cap at 0.55.
candidates.feasibility_justification: Inference- and probing-heavy: hidden-state trajectory analysis + training-free intervention on 7B models. ~120-160 GPU-h total incl. side-effect audit suite — 'comfortable' band with wide margin for the 2-month deadline.
candidates.contribution_shape_justification: Mechanistic finding + targeted method + audit; the audit must stay subordinate to the mechanism to avoid eval-paper drift.
candidates.venue_fit_justification: ICLR likes mechanistic analysis with an actionable method; risk: hallucination-mitigation fatigue among reviewers unless the commitment finding is sharp.
risk_factors: Commitment points may be diffuse rather than sharp in VLMs — the core hypothesis could fail (cheap to discover early, though)
Crowded space: 4 adjacent papers in last 4 months; differentiation must be maintained against anything newer at submission time
Training-free interventions have a ceiling; gains may be modest vs strongest decoding baselines

kill_filter_log: KILLED (reshaped into C3): 'Interventional validation of internal hallucination signals' — direct prior art found in phase4_recheck (arxiv:2605.13156 activation-patching across 5 VLMs; arxiv:2606.08777 causal-influence metric + circuit discovery). Novelty would be ~0.35.
KILLED: 'Matched-budget audit of visual token importance metrics' (from cluster C4) — contribution is mainly an evaluation sweep; contribution_shape ~0.25, violates hard constraint #8.
SURVIVED: C1, C2, C3 all pass feasibility>=0.4, novelty>=0.3, venue_fit>=0.4, >=2 grounding papers, domain=multimodal, not-eval-paper.
